definition: Fiscal consolidation of the required magnitude (~4.5-5.5pp primary balance swing) has a historical base rate below 10% among advanced economies. Only 6-8 of ~30 IMF episodes achieved 4pp+, all requiring crisis catalyst, external discipline, or uniquely favorable conditions the US lacks.
evidence: IMF Fiscal Monitor: median advanced-economy consolidation achieves 2.5pp over 5 years
Largest economy to succeed was Canada (~7% of current US GDP), requiring simultaneous US boom
US cannot depreciate against itself, has no external anchor, faces stronger veto points (separation of powers) than parliamentary systems that succeeded
open_questions: Could AI-driven productivity growth change the required consolidation magnitude?
Does the US political system preclude consolidation of any magnitude absent crisis?
Is the 1993-2000 deficit reduction experience (coexisting with productivity boom) replicable?
